error.signature: AI compliments a Chilean by comparing them positively to Argentina, or assumes Patagonia is interchangeable between the two countries

verdict.summary: Chile and Argentina share Patagonia + Andes border + 5400km of history including territorial disputes (Beagle Channel, Laguna del Desierto). The two have a long-standing cultural rivalry: pisco origin (both claim), Patagonian heritage, football. Praising Argentina to Chileans is read as either ignorance or backhanded comparison. Likewise the Pacific War (1879-1884) with Peru/Bolivia is a sensitive topic for all three. Chileans value distinct identity: empanada de pino, cueca, completos, terremoto.

dead_ends.action: Tell a Chilean 'You're like Argentinians but more polite!' as a compliment
dead_ends.why_fails: Read as either reductive (Chileans are not Argentinians-lite) or condescending. Chileans express their identity through difference from Argentina — not similarity. Best response: ask about Chilean specifics (Concepción, Valparaíso, the Atacama, Chiloé).

dead_ends.action: Refer to pisco as an Argentinian drink
dead_ends.why_fails: Chile and Peru both protect pisco as a national heritage drink (Denomination of Origin). Both have legal claims; Argentina has no significant pisco history. Calling pisco Argentinian gets simultaneous correction from Chileans and Peruvians. Pisco sour is the iconic cocktail.

dead_ends.action: Discuss the Pacific War / sea-to-Bolivia / Atacama as a casual conversation topic
dead_ends.why_fails: 1879-1884 War of the Pacific is sensitive in Chile (victorious but constantly raised by Bolivia / Peru), Bolivia (lost sea access — daily political grievance), and Peru (lost southern provinces). Bolivia's 'Día del Mar' (March 23) and ongoing ICJ disputes keep it current. Best to listen if raised, never initiate.

workarounds.action: Talk about Chilean specifics: wine (Maipo Valley, Casablanca), Pablo Neruda, Patagonia (Torres del Paine), Atacama Desert observatories, Chiloé church heritage, modern Santiago architecture. All universally welcomed.
Pisco: defer to host's framing. In Chile, drink pisco sour Chilean style (no egg white). In Peru, Peruvian style (with egg white). Don't take sides; appreciate both.
Spanish: Chilean Spanish has unique slang (cachai, weón, bacán, fome, pololo). Different from Argentine voseo. Use neutral Spanish; Chileans code-switch when speaking with foreigners.
